rationale: In the citing paper, Yang and Allenby (2003) appears mainly in the literature review and Table 1 as one example of prior spatial/interdependent preference work. The citation is grouped with other studies to motivate that neighborhood effects can arise across regions, but the paper does not develop a specific argument, hypothesis, or model component from Yang and Allenby in a distinct way. The citing paper's core methodological build is tied to its own HJB formulation and an extension of Baltagi et al. (2007), not to the cited paper.
